cards.back: A linear two-port model of the BJT for small-signal (AC) analysis. Four parameters: hie, hre, hfe, hoe.
Input impedance: hie = v1/i1 (v2=0). Typically 1-2 kΩ. Resistance looking into the base.
Forward current gain: hfe = i2/i1 (v2=0). AC β. Typically 50-200.

cards.front: hre and hoe — when to include?
cards.back: hre ≈ 10⁻⁴ (usually negligible). hoe: include if 1/hoe ≈ RC; ignore if 1/hoe >> 10·RC.
Only hie (input R) and hfe (current source). hre=0, hoe=0. Used when told 'simplified' model.
Input: hie + hre·v2 source. Output: hfe·i1 source || 1/hoe resistor.
CE: hie, hre, hfe, hoe. CC: hic, hrc, hfc, hoc. Relationship: hfc = −(1+hfe).
hfe = ΔIC/ΔIB at constant VCE. Read IC at two adjacent IB curves at the same VCE.
hoe = ΔIC/ΔVCE at constant IB. It's the slope of one IB curve at the Q-point.
Output resistance of the transistor. Appears in parallel with RC. Large (10-100kΩ) means nearly ideal current source.
